input:
REPO: SaptarshiSarkar12/Drifty
PR #801: chore: Upgrade Java to Version 23 and fix Build Issues
Changed files (12): .github/workflows/build.yml, .github/workflows/codeql.yml, .github/workflows/docker-publish.yml, .github/workflows/formatter.yml, .idea/misc.xml, CLI/pom.xml, Core/pom.xml, GUI/pom.xml, GUI/src/main/resources/META-INF/native-image/reachability-metadata.json, GUI/src/main/resources/META-INF/native-image/resource-config.json, docker-compose.yaml, pom.xml
Diff (+1851/-211):
PR DESCRIPTION:
## Fixes issue

<!-- If your PR fixes an open issue, use `Fixes #ISSUE_NUMBER` to link your PR with the issue. -->

Upgrades JDK to the latest version 23 and fixes the build errors for all three OS.

## Changes proposed

This pull request focuses on updating the Java Development Kit (JDK) version across various configuration files and improving the build setup for both CLI and GUI modes. The most important changes include updating the JDK version from 21 to 23 and configuring different setups for CLI and GUI modes for applying the fix.

## Check List (Check all the applicable boxes)

- [X] My code follows the code style of this project.
- [ ] My change requires changes to the documentation.
- [ ] I have updated the documentation accordingly.
- [X] This PR does not contain plagiarized content.
- [X] The title of my pull request is a short description of the requested changes.

## Screenshots

The latest Drifty GUI executable for Ubuntu has been tested and works flawlessly.
![Screenshot From 2025-03-04 10-20-47](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/692b9f5a-25b1-4a69-b870-8f01044fe6c0)
Other OS are not checked and any help in that regard (confirming whether the executables run and work as expected) would be appreciated :smile:.

DIFF:

--- .github/workflows/build.yml (+11/-2) ---
@@ -97,14 +97,23 @@ jobs:
         retry_wait_seconds: '30'
         command: |
           Core/src/main/resources/yt-dlp.exe -U
-    - name: Set up GraalVM JDK 21
+    - name: Set up GraalVM JDK 23
+      if: ${{ matrix.mode == 'CLI' }}
       uses: graalvm/setup-graalvm@v1
       with:
-        java-version: '21'
+        java-version: '23'
         distribution: 'graalvm'
         github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
         set-java-home: true
         cache: 'maven'
+    - name: Set up Gluon's GraalVM JDK 23
+      if: ${{ matrix.mode == 'GUI' }}
+      uses: gluonhq/setup-graalvm@master
+      env:
+        GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+      with:
+        arch: ${{ matrix.os == 'macos-14' && 'aarch64' || 'x86_64' }}
+        jdk: 'java23'
     - name: Package Drifty CLI for ${{ matrix.os }} with GraalVM
       if: ${{ matrix.mode == 'CLI' }}
       shell: bash
--- .github/workflows/codeql.yml (+2/-2) ---
@@ -30,12 +30,12 @@ jobs:
         languages: ${{ matrix.language }}
         queries: security-extended,security-and-quality
 
-    - name: Setup JDK 21
+    - name: Setup JDK 23
       if: ${{ matrix.language == 'java-kotlin' }}
       uses: actions/setup-java@v4
       with:
         distribution: 'oracle'
-        java-version: 21
+        java-version: 23
 
     - name: Build with Maven
       if: ${{ matrix.language == 'java-kotlin' }}
--- .github/workflows/docker-publish.yml (+11/-2) ---
@@ -74,14 +74,23 @@ jobs:
         run: |
           chmod +x Core/src/main/resources/yt-dlp
           Core/src/main/resources/yt-dlp -U
-      - name: Set up GraalVM JDK 21
+      - name: Set up GraalVM JDK 23
+        if: ${{ matrix.docker_context == 'CLI' }}
         uses: graalvm/setup-graalvm@v1
         with:
-          java-version: '21'
+          java-version: '23'
           distribution: 'graalvm'
           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
           set-java-home: true
           cache: 'maven'
+      - name: Set up Gluon's GraalVM JDK 23
+        if: ${{ matrix.docker_context == 'GUI' }}
+        uses: gluonhq/setup-graalvm@master
+        env:
+          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+        with:
+          arch: 'amd64'
+          jdk: 'java23'
       - name: Package Drifty CLI with GraalVM
         if: matrix.docker_context == 'CLI'
         run: mvn -P build-drifty-cli-for-ubuntu-latest package
--- .github/workflows/formatter.yml (+2/-2) ---
@@ -20,11 +20,11 @@ jobs:
         uses: actions/checkout@v4
         with:
           ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
-      - name: Setup JDK 21
+      - name: Setup JDK 23
         uses: actions/setup-java@v4
         with:
           distribution: 'oracle'
-          java-version: 21
+          java-version: 23
       - name: Format Java files
         run: mvn rewrite:run
       - name: Install prettier
--- .idea/misc.xml (+1/-1) ---
@@ -8,7 +8,7 @@
       </list>
     </option>
   </component>
-  <component name="ProjectRootManager" version="2" languageLevel="JDK_21" default="true" project-jdk-name="21" project-jdk-type="JavaSDK">
+  <component name="ProjectRootManager" version="2" languageLevel="JDK_23" default="true" project-jdk-name="23" project-jdk-type="JavaSDK">
     <output url="file://$PROJECT_DIR$/out" />
   </component>
 </project>
\ No newline at end of file
--- CLI/pom.xml (+2/-2) ---
@@ -14,8 +14,8 @@
     <description>CLI module of Drifty</description>
 
     <properties>
-        <maven.compiler.source>21</maven.compiler.source>
-        <maven.compiler.target>21</maven.compiler.target>
+        <maven.compiler.source>23</maven.compiler.source>
+        <maven.compiler.target>23</maven.compiler.target>
         <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
         <cli.main-class>main.Drifty_CLI</cli.main-class>
     </properties>
--- Core/pom.xml (+2/-2) ---
@@ -13,8 +13,8 @@
     <description>Core module of Drifty which includes the commonly used classes in Drifty CLI and GUI</description>
 
     <properties>
-        <maven.compiler.source>21</maven.compiler.source>
-        <maven.compiler.target>21</maven.compiler.target>
+        <maven.compiler.source>23</maven.compiler.source>
+        <maven.compiler.target>23</maven.compiler.target>
         <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
     </properties>
 
--- GUI/pom.xml (+7/-3) ---
@@ -13,8 +13,9 @@
     <description>GUI module of Drifty</description>
 
     <properties>
-        <maven.compiler.source>21</maven.compiler.source>
-        <maven.compiler.target>21</maven.compiler.target>
+        <maven.compiler.source>23</maven.compiler.source>
+        <maven.compiler.target>23</maven.compiler.target>
+        <gluonfx-maven-plugin.version>1.0.25</gluonfx-maven-plugin.version>
         <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
         <gui.main-class>main.Drifty_GUI</gui.main-class>
         <system-linker-arg/>
@@ -48,7 +49,7 @@
             <plugin>
                 <groupId>com.gluonhq</groupId>
                 <artifactId>gluonfx-maven-plugin</artifactId>
-                <version>1.0.23</version>
+
reference_review:
POINTS:
- [.github/workflows/docker-publish.yml:92] As executables in Docker images are currently released only for amd64 arch, this change is not necessary now.

POINTS:
- [plugin/trino-iceberg/src/main/java/io/trino/plugin/iceberg/IcebergUtil.java:1335] `file` cannot be null, pls remove that check
- [plugin/trino-iceberg/src/main/java/io/trino/plugin/iceberg/IcebergUtil.java:1338] It's guaranteed to be there, we should be using `checkState` to assert it and return

POINTS:
- [build.gradle:802] @gharris1727  without this ignoreFailures extension, check fails for almost any module I see.

input:
REPO: quarkiverse/quarkus-mcp-server
PR #51: fix: add metadata so sse/stdio shows up with description in code.quarkus and maven central
Changed files (4): transports/sse/pom.xml, transports/sse/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml, transports/stdio/pom.xml, transports/stdio/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml
Diff (+18/-1):
PR DESCRIPTION:
follow up to #26 which missed that the new sse and stdio extensions doesn't have any metadata so the descriptions are generic/wrong.

This fixes it - but does require new release.


DIFF:

--- transports/sse/pom.xml (+1/-1) ---
@@ -11,7 +11,7 @@
     <artifactId>quarkus-mcp-server-sse-parent</artifactId>
     <packaging>pom</packaging>
     <name>Quarkus MCP Server Transport - SSE - Parent</name>
-
+    <description>This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily using server side events (SSE).</description>
     <modules>
         <module>deployment</module>
         <module>runtime</module>
--- transports/sse/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml (+8/-0) ---
@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+name: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server for server side events (SSE)
+metadata:
+  keywords:
+    - mcp-server
+  guide: https://docs.quarkiverse.io/quarkus-mcp-server/dev/index.html
+  categories:
+    - "miscellaneous"
+  status: "preview"
--- transports/stdio/pom.xml (+1/-0) ---
@@ -11,6 +11,7 @@
     <artifactId>quarkus-mcp-server-stdio-parent</artifactId>
     <packaging>pom</packaging>
     <name>Quarkus MCP Server Transport - stdio - Parent</name>
+    <description>This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily using stanard input/output (stdio).</description>
 
     <modules>
         <module>deployment</module>
--- transports/stdio/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml (+8/-0) ---
@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+name: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server for standard input/output (stdio)
+metadata:
+  keywords:
+    - mcp-server
+  guide: https://docs.quarkiverse.io/quarkus-mcp-server/dev/index.html
+  categories:
+    - "miscellaneous"
+  status: "preview"
reference_review:
POINTS:
- [transports/sse/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml:5] ```suggestion
  guide: https://docs.quarkiverse.io/quarkus-mcp-server/dev/index.html
```

This guide is already published...
- [transports/stdio/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml:5] ```suggestion
  guide: https://docs.quarkiverse.io/quarkus-mcp-server/dev/index.html
```
- [transports/stdio/pom.xml:14] ```suggestion
    <description>This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily using standard input/output (stdio).</description>
```

POINTS:
- [test-support/spring-boot-test-support/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/testsupport/process/DisabledIfProcessUnavailableCondition.java:110] This is unrelated to falling back to an additional path. It's also unnecessary as `@DisabledIfProcessUnavailable` is never used with slashes in the command names as that would likely hurt their portability. Please revert.
- [core/spring-boot-docker-compose/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/docker/compose/core/ProcessRunner.java:140] This is unrelated to falling back to an additional path. It's also almost certainly unnecessary as ProcessRunner shouldn't be called with a command containing a `/`. Please revert.
- [buildpack/spring-boot-buildpack-platform/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/buildpack/platform/docker/configuration/CredentialHelper.java:120] This is unrelated to falling back to an additional path. It's also almost certainly unnecessary as the credential helper shouldn't have a `/` in its name. Please revert.

POINTS:
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:180] would be great to a have a test verifying it works
probably `ShowCreateUtilTest`

POINTS:
- [vertx-core/src/test/java/io/vertx/tests/eventbus/ClusteredEventBusTest.java:675] I think we do have with `MessageQueueOnWorkerThreadTest` which test in the case of worker when we had the issue. FWIW in my early implementation I actually reversed the order in which message are replied after cumulation in the new node selector implementation and the test did not fail.

I think the new test is better as it is because it introduces latency at the cluster manager level which is is what is likely to happen with message re-ordering.

POINTS:
- [agroal-pool/src/main/java/io/agroal/pool/util/AutoCloseableElement.java:20] I would write a couple of lines to describe the logic and invariants for each of the states involved here; for yourself of the future, including re ordering
